title: Theatrical versions
wikitext:
[]: Theatrical versions             Quote Post by Howl88 » Thu Jan 06, 2011 9:10 am Howdy everybody, not sure how active you all are but I really can't find the answer to my question anywhere else so I figure doing this is my best bett. Not sure how condemning you all are but when I found even a bootleg copy of Rebuild 1.0 without subtitles and all I jumped all over it and downloaded it so fast. Since then I've bought the DVD released 1.1 and 2.22 so in a way I've paid my dues. Heres the problem however, last September or so I caught a train with friends to Sydney purely to see Rebuild 2 in the theatre and of course it was absolutely amazing, months later I bought the 2.22 dvd and came to realise that I really didn't like the differences made in the revamp. For starters they totally downplay the musical scores in particular scenes, in the angel fight against SPOILER: Show

[]:             Quote Post by The Eva Monkey » Thu Jan 06, 2011 11:42 am Are you sure you're not just observing the difference between watching in a theater and watching at home? A theater's sound system is considerably better than what you have at home.

[]:             Quote Post by Clover » Thu Jan 06, 2011 2:20 pm The Eva Monkey wrote: Are you sure you're not just observing the difference between watching in a theater and watching at home? A theater's sound system is considerably better than what you have at home.

[]:             Quote Post by The Eva Monkey » Thu Jan 06, 2011 2:27 pm Clover wrote: You can tell from the cam rip that the music is crazy loud in the theatrical cut. It barely exists in 2.22. I might be able to do something about this, but it will take awhile.

[]:             Quote Post by A.T. Fish » Thu Jan 06, 2011 4:58 pm I'm not sure about this, but I think you can adjust the settings in a home theater and get the desired feeling, maybe turn the sound effects volume a bit lower? Or the soundtrack volume higher?

[]:             Quote Post by Baz » Thu Jan 06, 2011 5:49 pm A.T. Fish wrote: I'm not sure about this, but I think you can adjust the settings in a home theater and get the desired feeling, maybe turn the sound effects volume a bit lower? Or the soundtrack volume higher?

[]:             Quote Post by Clover » Thu Jan 06, 2011 6:25 pm The Eva Monkey wrote: You're basing this off of a lousy video recording from inside of a loud movie theater? Have you ever taken a microphone into a loud space and recorded? It sounds totally different than what you would expect to hear.

[]:             Quote Post by Howl88 » Fri Jan 07, 2011 6:12 am The Eva Monkey wrote: Are you sure you're not just observing the difference between watching in a theater and watching at home? A theater's sound system is considerably better than what you have at home.

[]:             Quote Post by JoeD80 » Fri Jan 07, 2011 5:56 pm I think it sounds better on my 2.22 BluRay. It seems to me that the music in that scene is meant to rise at points and lower at others for dramatic effect.

[]:             Quote Post by Kozar927 » Sat Jan 08, 2011 2:06 am I agree 100% I was very sad when I herd the lower music volume in the DVD release. The 2 best sceans in the movie were music sceans. And upon messing with different sound settings I couldn't give it a feel smiler to the theatrical release. I wish they didnt mess with it for the 2nd release

[]:             Quote Post by Warren Peace » Sat Jan 08, 2011 2:42 am I'm not sure anyone can draw any conclusions about the theater mix from the cam rip. The Funimation dub could turn out to have the best home mix, if they redo it from scratch and don't replicate the original levels. Who knows?
